[161:16]And I said from the outset, if the facts lead us to believe that there is some Israel connection.

[161:23]connection, any conspiracy. I said, I'm willing to go where the facts lead. I'm willing to

[161:29]follow. But from the very beginning, we had very little in the way of facts from the FBI.

[161:36]They were making some extraordinary claims. Law enforcement says we have videos, photos,

[161:43]written confession. We have DNA evidence. We have the murder weapon. We have a suspect.

[161:51]And it's important to keep in mind.

[161:54]In this assassination, we have a suspect in custody that the state claims is the killer.

[162:02]They've charged him.

[162:04]Unlike the Thomas Crooks, Donald Trump assassination attempt, they took him alive.

[162:11]What does that mean?

[162:16]in court. Thomas Crooks is dead. You can't convict him. So you don't have to worry about

[162:22]finding a jury. You don't have to worry about making the charge a stick. You don't have

[162:26]to worry about proving it to a jury. More importantly, you, you don't have to deal with

[162:32]that entire process because you don't have a suspect or a killer to try. This is different.

[162:40]the FBI has their man. Now, when you have a high profile killing like this, it is never

[162:47]the case that law enforcement releases all their information. It just isn't because,

[162:53]and the state came out, I think within days of the assassination, and they said something

[163:00]They said, we have a constitution.

[163:04]They said that the suspect has a fifth and sixth amendment right to a fair and speedy

[163:11]trial, to a jury of their peers.

[163:14]And I think what he was getting at, if we're being logical, is that if you're trying to

[163:22]convict this guy, you need to convict him before a jury.

[163:30]that is not tainted,

[163:33]that they don't know all the facts,

[163:35]they don't believe he's guilty.

[163:37]You need to find a dozen people in the state of Utah

[163:40]that do not know enough about the case

[163:43]that they're prejudiced against the suspect.

[163:46]They cannot taint the jury pool.

[163:49]So in an ordinary murder investigation,

[163:52]the state does not release everything to the public

[163:55]because the trial happens in court, not in the press.

[163:59]But in particular, in such a high profile case, you can't put out everything that you

[164:05]have because you need to find a jury and then you need to convict him in a court of law before

[164:10]a jury and allow

[164:45]literally will have their day in court.

[164:49]And then I assume those things eventually will make their way to the public, but it

[165:34]they have a guy, they claim to have a weapon,

[169:16]People are claiming that Charlie Kirk

[169:22]changing his position, his alignment. However you want to characterize it, for

[170:38]That is absolutely true.
